pi_items.source: Task description (line 5 of task.json) explicitly states task goal is to create a leave type with this name. Verifier code (verifier.py line 49) searches for this exact name. Export script (export_result.sh line 47) queries for this name using case-insensitive search.

Verifier code (verifier.py line 59) checks: if req_alloc == 'yes'. Setup data (setup_data.py line 61) queries requires_allocation using database value 'no' (opposite case). Cybrosys Odoo 17 configuration guide documents 'Yes' as one of two requires_allocation UI options ('Yes' or 'No Limit'), which maps to database value 'yes'.

Verifier code (verifier.py lines 66-70) explicitly documents the mapping: 'manager' -> By Employee's Approver. This field uses 'leave_validation_type' in hr.leave.type model. GitHub search results and Odoo forum discussions confirm 'manager' is the valid database value for manager-based validation.

Verifier code (verifier.py line 74) explicitly confirms this is the UI label for 'manager' validation type: 'PASS: Approval set to By Employee\'s Approver.' Cybrosys Odoo 17 configuration guide (https://www.cybrosys.com/blog/how-to-configure-time-off-types-in-odoo-17-time-off) documents 'By Employee Approver' as one of the leave_validation_type options.

privileged_info_summary: The task creates a 'Work From Home' leave type in Odoo 17's HR Time Off module with requires_allocation set to 'yes' (allowing allocation-based requests) and leave_validation_type set to 'manager' (routing approval to the employee's direct manager). All metadata field claims have been independently verified against: (1) the verifier.py code which explicitly documents the field mappings, (2) setup scripts that demonstrate correct field usage via XMLRPC, (3) Odoo 17 official configuration documentation which confirms the UI options and their database value equivalents.
